message:
fix(codex): deduplicate mirrored user turns in JSONL session files

Some Codex session files emit the same user message twice — once as a
`response_item` with `type: "message", role: "user"` and again as an
`event_msg` with `type: "user_message"`. Both entries share the same
timestamp and identical content, but the parser previously treated them
as separate messages. This broke the expected user-assistant alternation
pattern, inflated message counts, and could confuse downstream
consumers during session conversion.

The fix checks whether the message about to be pushed is an adjacent
duplicate of the previous message (same role=User, same content, same
timestamp) before appending it. This is applied in both the
`response_item` and `event_msg` parsing branches so deduplication works
regardless of which entry appears first in the file.

A new unit test (`reader_jsonl_dedupes_mirrored_user_entries`) verifies
that a four-line JSONL file with mirrored user entries produces exactly
two canonical messages (one user, one assistant) instead of three.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
